title: crepis occidentalis subsp. pumila
intro: Crepis occidentalis subsp. pumila, also known as dwarf western hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Crepis occidentalis subsp. pumila can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for use in herbal medicine.
